title: Error Parsing SystemVerilog
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Below it is the transcript from my command line, just try to synth the dff and the register based in the dff, described using SV .


## Expected behavior

*Please describe the behavior you would have expected from the tool.*
the following should be observed:
 yosys

 /----------------------------------------------------------------------------\
 |                                                                            |
 |  yosys -- Yosys Open SYnthesis Suite                                       |
 |                                                                            |

 |  ANY SPECIAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES    |
 |  WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN     |
 |  ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT OF   |
 |  OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.            |
 |                                                                            |
 \----------------------------------------------------------------------------/

 Yosys 0.7+663 (git sha1 799c4248, clang 6.0.1-svn334776-1~exp1~20180830180435.102 -fPIC -Os)


yosys> read_verilog -sv ../src/sv/dff.sv 
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `../src/sv/dff.sv' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\dff'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

yosys> read_verilog -sv ../src/sv/register.sv 

2. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `../src/sv/register.sv' to AST representation.
../src/sv/register.sv:4: ERROR: syntax error
[files.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2398719/files.zip)

## Actual behavior

It should merely synthesize the file. Despite I don't expect the tool to support the entirety of SystemVerilog fully, however, I do expect it to be able to parse any SV file.
